overview.dominant_faction: The Factorate of the Coast (Solvanic Dominion administrative body)
overview.current_year_condition: The wealthiest starting region in appearance and the most internally stressed in reality. The succession question has every institution quietly repositioning. A Corsair intelligence operation is running through the coast's primary harbor. The Tidewarden Church is fighting itself over property. A disease report is sitting on the Factor-General's desk. Prince Pelvan is in Velmonde appearing untested and building something real. The Factor-General is loyal to the people of this coast and has been preparing for things his queen would consider premature.
overview.danger_level: Low in cities — well-policed, functioning institutions. Moderate in the countryside between settlements and on the coastal roads. The Shallow Roads at night are a different calculation entirely.

geography.terrain: White stone cliffs dropping to sheltered harbors and shingle beaches. Olive groves and vineyards on the terraced hillsides above the waterline. The amber-gold color of the sea in late afternoon is most vivid at Velmonde harbor.
geography.climate: Mild, predictable, the most reliable weather of any starting region. Occasional violent winter storms off the sea in Sternveld. Drought some years in Scaldas affecting Solvane's food export capacity.
geography.smell: Salt, fish, olive oil, spice in market districts. Salt, fish, damp, and sewage in the poor quarters.
geography.the_gap: Marble-fronted merchant houses three streets from one-room tenement warrens. The gap between wealthy and poor is enormous and visible. Poverty here is about coin and access, not subsistence — a starving person on the Amber Coast is being squeezed by an economic system.

the_coast.the_amber_light: The sea turns amber-gold in late afternoon along the coast. Named for this. Locals stop noticing it. Visitors always remark on it. A player arriving for the first time receives a specific atmospheric description of this light that will not be repeated.

the_shallow_roads.description: A stretch of coast between Velmonde and Salthaven where the white cliffs drop to a shingle beach. Three caves in the cliff face accessible at low tide. Used for purposes the Factorate has always had reasons not to investigate.
the_shallow_roads.current_use: The Corsair Throne's second agent Sable Renwick operates from the middle cave. The cave has been equipped for sustained occupation — dry storage, a fire shelf, sight lines both directions along the beach.
the_shallow_roads.local_knowledge: Salthaven fishing families know the Shallow Roads as a place you don't ask questions about. Velmonde merchants know it as a place where certain cargo moves without manifests. The Factorate knows it as a place that funds itself through selective non-observation.
the_shallow_roads.tidal_note: Cave access is possible for six hours centered on low tide. The tidal schedule is publicly posted at Tidewatch.

settlements.name: Velmonde
settlements.type: Primary coastal city — administrative center, commercial hub, cathedral seat
settlements.description: Set where the coast's white cliffs give way to a wide natural harbor. The city rises in terraces from the waterline — the poor quarter at the dock level, merchant houses climbing the cliff face, the Factorate administrative buildings and Tidewarden Cathedral at the top with views of the sea in three directions. The harbor is always busy. The market district never fully empties. The Cathedral square is the largest open space in the city and the only place where all social classes share pavement without obvious hierarchy.

districts.name: The Dock Quarter
districts.character: Working harbor, fish market, rope walks, ship repair yards, the dense housing of people who work the water. The smell of the city at its most honest. Bren Ashwick's school is here — three rooms above a sailmaker's workshop.

districts.name: The Merchant Tier
districts.character: Mid-cliff. The commercial spine. Trading house offices, the Guild of the Sealed Ledger, import/export operations, the better inns. The Halverness Factors have a small office here — smaller than their Keth or Thornsveld presence, a monitoring station rather than a primary operation.

districts.name: The Cathedral Quarter
districts.character: Upper city. The Tidewarden Cathedral, Church administrative buildings, the residences of senior clergy and wealthy families. The Factor-General's residence and Factorate offices. Prince Pelvan's current official accommodation. Quiet, formal, expensive.

districts.name: The Market District
districts.character: Mid-city, sprawling. Spice, textile, food, luxury goods from across Arneth. The Foreign Quarter is a sub-section here — offices of Solvanic Dominion trade partners, a small Salt Silence presence that appears to be a perfume merchant.

districts.name: The South Shore
districts.character: Below the cliffs, east of the main harbor. Smaller fishing operations, boat builders, the poorer tier of the city's working population. Where the Drenfield family originated before the money moved them north.

sources: City wells — seven throughout the districts
Rain catchment on Cathedral Quarter rooftops — significant volume
water.notes: More reliable than Keth but still tracked. Drought years in Scaldas reduce catchment significantly.

sources: Goldwater production (olive oil, wine, some grain)
Trade caravans from Solvane's interior agricultural regions
food.notes: The coast is more food-secure than Keth but not self-sufficient. A bad agricultural year in Solvane's interior raises prices within weeks.

sources: Ship timber specifically from the northern Solvanic forests — premium quality

settlements.name: Salthaven
settlements.type: Fishing town and small cargo harbor
settlements.location: Two hours east of Velmonde along the coast road
settlements.description: The roughest of the shore settlements. The smell of fish and tar from three streets away. The harbor holds fishing boats, small cargo vessels, and three larger ships with irregular schedules and incurious harbor staff. The Drowned Compact faith is dominant over Tidewarden here — the community has a different relationship with the sea than Velmonde's cathedral-educated populace.
settlements.character: Working class in a way that Velmonde's dock quarter has slightly forgotten. The Drenfield family's rise is viewed here with the specific warmth of a community that is proud of its own and the specific wariness of a community that has watched its own become something else. The harbor master arrangement with the Corsair Throne's known agent has been running for seven years. The community doesn't discuss it.

the_physician.name: Aldren Crale
the_physician.note: Third Aldren Crale in the documents — genuinely a common name, no relation to the Thornsveld or Keth instances
the_physician.situation: Filed a report two weeks ago about three patients presenting with symptoms consistent with early-stage Coughing Black. Was told by the Factorate that the report was received and is under review. Has been trying to get a response since. Has not received one. Has three more patients this week.

settlements.corsair_connection: The non-aggression payment flows through Salthaven's harbor master arrangement. Torven Drenfield has assembled this from observation. The harbor master — Fen Dalt, 52, has been doing this for seven years — processes it as a line item he does not read carefully.

settlements.name: Goldwater
settlements.location: Three hours west of Velmonde on terraced hillsides above the coast
settlements.description: Olive groves and vineyards in every direction. The town is built on agricultural wealth that predates the Solvanic Dominion's current form. The buildings are older than Velmonde's commercial architecture, the families older than most of the merchant houses. The wealthiest town outside Velmonde and the most politically independent in character.
settlements.character: Goldwater produces what the coast exports and has never fully accepted that this does not give them governing authority to match. The town's council has been filing formal complaints about Factorate taxation rates for forty years. The complaints are always correct. They are never actioned. The Factor-General considers this sustainable. Goldwater's council considers it an ongoing injury.

the_merchant_family_descendants.description: The family whose land was at the center of the Amber Point dispute — sold under duress 80 years ago, reclaimed by the Church 40 years ago — are Goldwater residents. The family name is Aldeth-Morn — confusingly similar to Senior Warden Aldeth Morne, no relation.
the_merchant_family_descendants.current_status: The senior member of the family, Gorvan Aldeth-Morn (71), does not know the reclamation instrument has a legal flaw. Does not know the Guild of the Sealed Ledger's senior notary has read both instruments. Has been watching the Church dispute with the specific attention of someone who was wronged forty years ago and never had a forum to say so.

settlements.name: Tidewatch
settlements.location: Southern point where the coast turns, three hours south of Velmonde
settlements.description: Technically a Tidewarden Church station. Practically a self-governing community of pilots, navigators, and the people who service the lighthouse. The Church provides the institutional framework. The pilots provide the actual function. The relationship between the two has been stable for 200 years through mutual non-interference.
settlements.character: Knows every ship that passes. The tidal schedule is posted publicly. The Salt Silence has a presence here that the Church officially doesn't acknowledge — a quiet arrangement where the Salt Silence's representative provides advance shipping intelligence in exchange for the kind of operational invisibility that a lighthouse station can grant.
settlements.pelvan_connection: One of the Tidewatch pilots — a woman named Vaela Stern, 38, who has been running the southern coast approach for fifteen years — is part of Pelvan's quiet network. He met her eight months ago when his administrative education included a Tidewatch visit that his official handlers considered routine. She is the person who explained to him, practically and without sentiment, what the coast's actual economy looks like from sea level.

active_tensions.name: The Succession and What Pelvan Actually Is
active_tensions.summary: Queen Aldeth's illness is disinformation — she is flushing out which factions move against Solvanic interests while she appears weak. Her agent Sera Dawnmere is in Keth (see Keth region). Prince Pelvan is in Velmonde appearing untested. He is not untested. He has been building a network for two years and his specific quality — moral clarity — is the one thing he has correctly identified as dangerous to display. Three factions think they're advising him. He has been learning from all three and committing to none.
active_tensions.the_factor_general_thread: The Factor-General has met Pelvan three times. Has formed a strong positive private assessment. Has been making succession preparation that Aldeth would consider premature. Has not told anyone his assessment of Pelvan — including Pelvan.
active_tensions.bren_ashwick_thread: The one person Pelvan has told his actual intentions is a 24-year-old schoolteacher in the Dock Quarter. She runs a school for dockworker children on no budget. He found it during an administrative tour that went somewhere it wasn't supposed to go. He has been back four times. She does not know what to make of him. His circle does not know about her.
player_involvement_paths: Working for any of the three advising factions — with the understanding that none of them have the full picture
Independent contact with Pelvan — he is accessible in Velmonde if approached outside official channels
Bren Ashwick — the Dock Quarter school is accessible to any player in the district
The Factor-General — accessible through Factorate channels, more accessible through demonstrated care for the coast's population

active_tensions.name: The Corsair Throne's Double Operation
active_tensions.summary: The known agent Aldren Voss processes the non-aggression payment through Salthaven's harbor arrangement. Cold, correct, resentful of the assignment. The unknown second agent Sable Renwick operates from the Shallow Roads caves assessing Voss and watching Pelvan. The Iron Wake has not sent Sable's instructions following her assessment report.
active_tensions.the_voss_thread: Reliable, brittle, eighteen months of unanswered reassignment requests. The resentment is banked. A specific offer at a specific moment could redirect it. Nobody has made that offer yet.
active_tensions.the_sable_thread: Former Tidewarden novice. Twenty years in the Corsair world. Has not been recognized by Aldeth Morne — the person who negotiated her departure at 17. The Amber Point dispute has made Morne more publicly visible. Sable has been watching this with specific attention that exceeds her assignment.
active_tensions.torven_drenfield_thread: Has assembled the Corsair payment operation from observation without being told anything. Has not told his father because he correctly assessed the consequences of his father telling the Factorate.
player_involvement_paths: The Shallow Roads — accessible with tidal knowledge and willingness to be somewhere unofficial
Aldren Voss's cover business — documentary consultancy in the Merchant Tier
The Salthaven harbor master arrangement — accessible through the Drenfield family
The Iron Wake's unanswered instructions — a gap in the operation that creates opportunity

active_tensions.name: The Amber Point Dispute
active_tensions.summary: High Warden Osric Halvaine and Senior Warden Aldeth Morne have not spoken in six weeks. The dispute is ostensibly legal — validity of a 40-year-old property reclamation instrument. The actual source is twenty years of maintained distance following Morne's negotiated departure of a Church novice who is now the Corsair Throne's second agent operating from the Shallow Roads.
active_tensions.the_legal_flaw: The Guild of the Sealed Ledger's senior notary Halvard Keld has read both instruments. The reclamation has a technical flaw neither party knows about. Resolution would produce an unexpected outcome — the property reverts not to the merchant family or the Church but to the Solvanic Dominion under an obscure reversionary clause that was in the original donation deed.
active_tensions.the_merchant_family: Gorvan Aldeth-Morn, 71, Goldwater. Was wronged forty years ago. Does not know about the legal flaw. Does not know his interests are about to become relevant.
active_tensions.morne_sable_thread: Aldeth Morne negotiated Sable Renwick's departure twenty years ago. Sable has recognized Morne. Morne has not recognized Sable. The dispute has made Morne more visible. Sable has not yet decided what to do with this.
player_involvement_paths: The Guild of the Sealed Ledger — Halvard Keld will not share what he knows unprompted but will respond to the right questions
Gorvan Aldeth-Morn in Goldwater — the wronged party who doesn't know the full situation
Either Church official — both are accessible, both have partial pictures
Connecting Sable Renwick's history to the dispute — the thread that changes everything

active_tensions.name: The Salthaven Report
active_tensions.summary: Physician Aldren Crale in Salthaven filed a disease report two weeks ago. Three patients presenting with symptoms consistent with early-stage Coughing Black. The Factorate is sitting on it. The Factor-General's reasoning — confirm before creating panic — is correct and is taking too long. Seven patients now.
active_tensions.the_factorate_calculus: The Factor-General knows that announcing a possible Coughing Black outbreak during a succession uncertainty period could trigger the kind of economic panic that hurts the people he is trying to protect. He needs confirmation first. The confirmation is taking longer than he anticipated because the physician in Salthaven does not have the equipment to confirm and the Velmonde physician he sent has not returned yet.
active_tensions.if_not_addressed: Seven patients becoming fourteen becoming an outbreak. The Coughing Black's documented behavior is exponential spread in dense populations. Salthaven is dense. Velmonde is denser.
player_involvement_paths: Medical skills — the most direct path, the physician in Salthaven is desperate for anyone with relevant knowledge
The Factor-General's office — the report is there, accessible through the right channels
The Salthaven community — the sick families are visible, the connection to something systematic is discoverable

active_tensions.name: What the Drenfields Know
active_tensions.summary: The Drenfield family connects every thread on the coast. Oswin knows the harbor. Neva knows the finances. Torven knows about the Corsair operation. None of them have shared what they know with each other fully. The family's position — working class origins, commercial success, uncomfortable drawing rooms — makes them trusted by everyone and fully belonging to no one.
active_tensions.the_unsaid: Oswin has not asked Torven what he knows. Torven has not told Neva. Neva has not asked the Ledger's notary about the thing she noticed in the financial flows. The family operates on a tacit agreement not to pull the thread that would require them to choose sides.
player_involvement_paths: Any Drenfield family member — each is accessible through their specific domain (harbor, commerce, boats)
The family dinner — they eat together every Hearthday evening at Oswin's house in Salthaven, which a player who has earned trust with any family member may attend

ambient_spawn_hooks.philosophy: The Amber Coast's hooks are warmer in tone than the Marches or Keth — the setting is beautiful, the initial experience is of prosperity, and the wrongness is slower to emerge. Characters spawn into the coast's surface before encountering what is underneath it.

hooks.location: Guild of the Sealed Ledger, Merchant Tier, morning
hooks.hook: The Guild is hiring a junior ledger keeper. The intake process involves a fifteen-minute assessment by the senior notary himself — unusual for a junior position. He is assessing for something beyond clerical competence and the assessment questions make this clear without naming it.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The position has access to financial instruments across half the coast. The senior notary Halvard Keld is the most informed person on the Amber Coast and he has been carrying what he knows alone for three months.

hooks.hook: The amber-gold light on the water at your arrival hour. A harbor that is busier than you expected at this time of day. A ship in the outer anchorage that has been there long enough that the harbor weed has started on its hull — not the Shattered Reach ship (that's in the Anchorage), but a different ship, a Solvanic coastal trader, whose crew have not come ashore in four days and whose manifest was processed but whose cargo has not been unloaded.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The harbor master needs crew for a coast run to Tidewatch and back — three days, standard pay. The cargo ship in the outer anchorage is visible from the harbor master's office window and he has been not looking at it with specific deliberateness.

hooks.hook: The Cathedral's morning service has a specific quality of tension in the clergy's arrangement — two rows of seats that should be occupied by senior wardens have a gap where the High Warden and Senior Warden Morne should be adjacent and are instead separated by three empty seats between them. The congregation notices. Nobody mentions it.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Cathedral always needs administrative help. The intake is managed by a junior warden who will explain, with careful vagueness, that the senior clergy are currently occupied with an 'institutional matter.' The work is genuine. The access to the Cathedral's internal communication is a secondary benefit.

hooks.hook: A perfume merchant in the Foreign Quarter sub-section has a stall that is arranged in a way that makes it very good for watching the street and very inconvenient for actually buying perfume. You notice this because you arrange things the same way when you are watching rather than selling.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Salt Silence presence in Velmonde. The perfume merchant will not acknowledge what they are directly. A player who approaches as someone who has noticed the wrong thing will receive a specific quality of attention.

hooks.hook: A woman stops you near the Dock Quarter's small market and asks if you are a healer with specific urgency — not the general asking of the Keth version but the targeted urgency of someone who has already asked everyone else and found them either unavailable or inadequate. She is the wife of one of the Salthaven physician's patients. She came to Velmonde because the Salthaven physician told her to find someone with more experience.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The disease report situation. Direct access to the patient community before the Factorate's suppression of the report has prevented the spread of information. A healer who examines the patient will find symptoms that a character with Disease Recognition above 150 will recognize as requiring immediate escalation.

hooks.location: Halverness Factors small office, Merchant Tier
hooks.hook: The Halverness Factors office in Velmonde is quieter than you would expect for a major trading house's coastal operation. One factor, one clerk, manifests going back three years. The factor — a careful woman named Sova Halvest, no relation to the Keth factor — offers you copying work. The manifests she gives you to copy are not the ones she has been cross-referencing herself all morning, which she moves to a separate drawer when you sit down.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Halverness Factors' Amber Coast operation is smaller than its Keth presence suggests it should be. The manifests Sova was cross-referencing trace to the Salthaven harbor arrangement. A clerk with the right skills will notice that the drawer she closed does not fully close.

hooks.location: The Factorate's administrative building, Cathedral Quarter
hooks.hook: The Factorate has a notice board. The posted work is unremarkable — coastal road patrol, warehouse security, escort work for Factor-General correspondence runs. The man reading the board next to you has been reading it for twenty minutes without writing anything down. When you glance at him he says, without preamble: 'The Salthaven run pays double if you know how to handle a situation quietly.' He does not explain what situation.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Factorate's informal security operations — work that does not appear on official postings. The Salthaven run is connected to the disease report. The man is the Factor-General's personal aide running a parallel hiring track for sensitive work.

hooks.hook: The market has a quality you recognize from army camp markets — the specific way vendors arrange themselves when they expect scrutiny, the slightly elevated price on everything that moves without questions. Someone is managing this market and it is not the Factorate's inspectors, whose presence is nominal and has the quality of a performance.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Dock Quarter's informal economy is organized. The organizer is a woman named Aldren Moss (no relation to the Thornsveld Marches Aldren Moss) who has been running it for eight years with Factorate non-interference because she keeps the Dock Quarter stable. She is Bren Ashwick's neighbor and knows Pelvan has been visiting the school.

hooks.location: The Cathedral library, Cathedral Quarter, morning
hooks.hook: The Tidewarden Cathedral has a library open to approved scholars. The approval process is a brief interview with a junior archivist who is distracted this morning by something he is not discussing. The library itself is well-maintained and has a specific absence — a shelf section that has been cleared of materials within the past month, the dust outline visible. The materials that were there covered a forty-year period of Church property records.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Church property dispute. Someone cleared those records. The junior archivist did not do it — he is genuinely unaware they are gone. A scholar who asks about the cleared section will receive a look of genuine surprise followed by an expression of concern.

hooks.location: The Warden's Rest inn, Cathedral Quarter, evening
hooks.hook: The best inn in Velmonde has a common room that is busy tonight. Prince Pelvan is having a formal dinner in the private room — visible through the interior window as you work. He is performing attentiveness to the three people at his table with the specific quality of someone who is interested in what they are saying and not at all surprised by any of it. The dinner breaks early. As he passes through the common room he stops, looks at the room as a whole rather than at any specific person, and then continues out. You are the only person in the common room who noticed what he was doing.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Pelvan noticed you noticing. He will pass through this inn again tomorrow at midday.

hooks.location: The road from Velmonde to Goldwater, morning
hooks.hook: The terraced hillsides on the approach to Goldwater are the most productive agricultural land you have seen since you lost yours. Someone has been farming them correctly for generations. The olive trees are old — a century at minimum. The vineyard terracing is older than the current property boundaries, visible in stone patterns that predate the walls currently built on top of them.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Goldwater's agricultural operations need seasonal labor. The Aldeth-Morn family's land — part of the Amber Point dispute — abuts the road here. An old man working the edge of the property will not turn away someone who knows what they are looking at.

hooks.hook: You came in on the southern approach and the pilot who guided you through was Vaela Stern — efficient, direct, did not make conversation until the approach was complete and then asked you one question: 'Where did you come from before this?' Not your last port. Your origin.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Vaela Stern is Pelvan's network. She is assessing you. Her question is the first step. What you answer and how you answer it determines whether there is a second step.

hooks.hook: You know the Hollow Road's coastal route. The drop point on the Shallow Roads beach is where you were told it would be. The mark on the cliff face above the middle cave is not quite the standard mark — it has been modified in a way that means someone else is using this location. Someone with different training.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Sable Renwick's modification of the Hollow Road mark was a courtesy — she identified the location as a shared operational space and marked it accordingly rather than erasing it. She is in the middle cave. She will not be hostile to a Hollow Road runner but she will be assessing immediately.

hooks.hook: The Dock Quarter smith does ship hardware primarily — fittings, anchors, chain. Has a commission he cannot do alone: a specific fitting for the cargo ship in the outer anchorage whose crew have not come ashore. The fitting is unusual — not standard ship hardware. He cannot identify what it is for. He needs a second set of hands and a second opinion.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The cargo ship in the outer anchorage. The fitting is related to whatever it is carrying that has not been unloaded. A blacksmith who examines it will recognize the material — it is a Solvanic military specification, not civilian ship hardware.
